commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings be eaten at all on the third day, it shall not be accepted, neither shall it be imputed unto him that offers it: it shall be an abomination, and the soul that eats of it shall bear his iniquity. Hebrew, "it is an abomination to be thrown away "and the soul Thus by neglecting to comply exactly with God's commands, we lose the fruits of our former piety. (Haydock) The flesh of these victims might be eaten in any clean place, by all those who were not defiled, chap. (Josephus) (Tirinus).
